User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user account within the SyncDoc application. This entity stores basic user information but excludes sensitive authentication details like passwords, which are handled by an external authentication system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The user's unique email address, used for login and notifications.

name.description: The user's display name or full name.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was last updated.

Document.title: Document
Document.description: Represents a collaborative document in the SyncDoc workspace, containing its content, metadata, and owner information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Document entity.

title.description: The title or name of the document.

content.description: The rich text content of the document, typically stored in a structured format like TipTap/ProseMirror JSON.

ownerId.description: Reference to the User who created and owns this document. (Relationship: User 1:N Document)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the document was initially created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the document content or metadata was last updated.

Permission.title: Permission
Permission.description: Manages access control for documents, linking users to specific documents with defined roles (Owner, Editor, Viewer).

id.description: Unique identifier for the Permission entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who is granted a specific role for the document. (Relationship: User 1:N Permission)

documentId.description: Reference to the Document to which the permission applies. (Relationship: Document 1:N Permission)

role.description: The access role assigned to the user for this document (e.g., 'Owner', 'Editor', 'Viewer').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this permission record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this permission record was last updated.

Message.title: Message
Message.description: Stores individual chat messages sent within a document's real-time chat sidebar.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Message entity.

documentId.description: Reference to the Document in which this message was sent. (Relationship: Document 1:N Message)

senderId.description: Reference to the User who sent this message. (Relationship: User 1:N Message)

content.description: The textual content of the chat message. Can be empty if only a file is attached to the message.

fileId.description: Optional reference to a File entity if this message includes or announces a file upload. (Relationship: File 1:1 Message)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the message was sent.

File.title: File
File.description: Represents a file (e.g., image, PDF) uploaded and shared within a document's workspace, with its Cloudinary URL and metadata.

id.description: Unique identifier for the File entity.

documentId.description: Reference to the Document where this file was uploaded. (Relationship: Document 1:N File)

uploaderId.description: Reference to the User who uploaded this file. (Relationship: User 1:N File)

url.description: The public URL where the file is stored (e.g., on Cloudinary).

filename.description: The original name of the uploaded file.

fileType.description: The MIME type or category of the file (e.g., 'image/jpeg', 'application/pdf').

size.description: The size of the file in bytes.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the file was uploaded.

definition.description: User profiles. Each user's document is owned by themselves (request.auth.uid == userId). This collection primarily stores user metadata required for profile management and identification across the application.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching request.auth.uid.

definition.description: Collaborative documents. This document includes a denormalized 'members' map (e.g., 'members: { [userId]: "role" }') for robust, authorization-independent access control. This map is the primary source for determining user roles (Owner, Editor, Viewer) and enables secure list queries (QAPs). The 'ownerId' field indicates the creator, who is always an 'Owner' in the 'members' map.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the document.

definition.description: Records individual user access grants to documents. This collection is primarily managed by backend services which synchronize these records with the 'members' map on the respective Document entity. To ensure authorization independence for write operations on this collection, each Permission document includes a denormalized 'documentOwnerId' (the owner of the associated document).

params.name: permissionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the permission record.

definition.description: Real-time chat messages within a specific document. Each message document includes a denormalized 'documentMembers' map (copied from the parent document) for authorization independence. This allows security rules to check sender permissions (Editor/Owner for write, all members for read) directly on the message document without a parent 'get()' call.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent document.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique identifier for the chat message.

definition.description: Files (images, PDFs) uploaded to a specific document. Each file document includes a denormalized 'documentMembers' map (copied from the parent document) for authorization independence. This ensures that rules for file uploads (Editor/Owner) and access (all members) can be enforced directly on the file document itself without a parent 'get()' call.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent document.

params.name: fileId
params.description: The unique identifier for the uploaded file.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed following the core principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and Access Modeling, ensuring a secure, scalable, and debuggable system for SyncDoc. 

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**

1.  **`/documents/{documentId}` (Document):** This collection is central to collaboration. To achieve Authorization Independence and avoid costly `get()` calls in security rules, each `Document` document includes a denormalized `members` map (e.g., `members: { 'userId1': 'Owner', 'userId2': 'Editor' }`). This map stores the roles of all users with access to the document. Security rules can directly check `resource.data.members[request.auth.uid]` to determine a user's permissions, making access checks atomic and highly efficient.
2.  **`/permissions/{permissionId}` (Permission):** While the `Document.members` map is the primary source of truth for real-time access control, the `Permission` entity exists as a separate record (as required by the prompt's database table definition) to manage these grants. For its own write operations, each `Permission` document denormalizes the `documentOwnerId` from the related `Document`. This allows security rules for `permissions` to check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.documentOwnerId` directly, without a `get()` call to the `Document` collection, thereby preserving Authorization Independence for this collection. Backend services would be responsible for synchronizing changes in this collection with the `Document.members` map.
3.  **`/documents/{documentId}/messages/{messageId}` (Message):** As a subcollection of `Document`, each `Message` document denormalizes the `documentMembers` map from its parent `Document`. This critical denormalization allows security rules for `Message` documents to check `resource.data.documentMembers[request.auth.uid]` directly. For instance, to read messages, a user only needs to be present in `documentMembers`, while to write, they need an 'Editor' or 'Owner' role. This eliminates `get()` calls to the parent `Document` for every message operation.
4.  **`/documents/{documentId}/files/{fileId}` (File):** Similar to messages, each `File` document within its subcollection also denormalizes the `documentMembers` map from its parent `Document`. This ensures that rules for file uploads (only 'Editor' or 'Owner' can upload) and file access (all members can read) can be evaluated directly on the `File` document itself, without needing `get()` calls to its parent `Document`.

**QAPs (Query-Based Access Patterns) Support:**

*   **`/documents`:** The denormalized `members` map enables secure `list` operations. Users can efficiently query for all documents they have access to by using a query like `db.collection('documents').where('members.' + request.auth.uid, 'exists')` or `db.collection('documents').where('members.' + request.auth.uid, 'in', ['Owner', 'Editor', 'Viewer'])`. This ensures that only authorized documents are returned, satisfying the QAPs principle.
*   **`/users/{userId}`:** User profiles are directly accessible by their UID, simplifying individual user data retrieval.
*   **Subcollections (`/messages`, `/files`):** Once a user has established access to a parent document (verified by the `Document.members` map and subsequently denormalized to subcollection documents), listing children entities (messages, files) within that document context becomes secure and straightforward. Rules ensure that only document members can perform read operations, and specific roles can perform write operations on these subcollections.

This architecture minimizes rule complexity, maximizes security, and optimizes for atomic operations by making each document independently auditable for access control.
